observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to notify in writing the payees of the stale checks, at least one month before the checks become stale, and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare JEV to adjust the Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts for the amounts of the stale checks, to reflect the correct account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct:
The Inventory Committee to conduct an annual physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a Report on Physical Count of PPE to be submitted to COA not later than January 31 of each year;
The Municipal Accountant to update the ledger cards for each class and category of property and equipment; and
The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain Property Cards and ensures reconciliation against the Accounting ledgers cards.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the inventory committee to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less and to prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities, and ensure full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the provision of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and Section COA Circular 2012-002 and incorporate/include under separate caption the list of projects and activities charged to the previous years’ unexpended LDRRMF in the ensuing year’s LDRRMFIP.
We recommended that Management properly address the issues and concerns that hindered the implementation of these projects so that PPAs can all be implemented and carried out as planned.

We recommended that Management direct the:
The Municipal Engineer to require the contractors to prepare and submit the As-Built Plan for completed projects prior to issuance of Certificate of Completion and posting of warranty security to guarantee their performance in the event of eventual structural defects/failures; and
The Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers relating to procurement of goods and infrastructure projects are adequately supported with mandatory documentary requirements before processing the payments.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take-home pay for government employees to boost the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.

We recommended that Management require the following:
MHO to submit copies of DTRs and accomplishment reports, along with the copies of medical journals to support the claim of SRA. Further, submit the approved working arrangements and area of assignment of the eligible BHW, HRHs and public HWs for AHDP and SRA grant .
Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation as provided by Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

observations.reason:
Not Implemented

Per post audit, the Municipal Engineer was not able to provide a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to the COA Office.

Not Implemented

Per post audit, there are no disclosures for the depreciation and impairment, and additions and disposals for Property, Plant and Equipment in the Notes to Financial Statements.

Partially Implemented

Per post audit, few PPE items were not recognized in the Financial Statements of the LGU.

Partially Implemented

Per audit, the submission of daily time records to those personnel who did not submit the same and the alternative work arrangements during the period of ECQ was not submitted in the Office of the auditor.

Partially Implemented

The Municipal Treasurer still did not furnish the accounting unit with the certified list of taxpayers with the stated amount due and collectible for the year as basis in setting-up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of each year.

Partially Implemented

The four mandatory position was still not filled up. The MDRRM Officer was still designated to the Municipal Agriculturist of LGU Bayabas.

Partially Implemented

The COA Office still waiting for the submission of the required documents for further verification.

observations.recommendation:
Requires the Municipal Engineer to provide a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
For existing road networks, directs the Municipal Assessor to assist the Municipal Engineer in the assessment of the fair value of the road lot component of local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipal Government.
Directs the Municipal Accountant to recognize the amount pertaining to the road lot as part of the reported book value of the Road Networks account.

We recommended and agreed that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to provide full disclosure of the accounts in the Notes to the Financial Statements as required by the GAM for LGUs. Additionally, install an accounting system that will enhance the efficiency of financial reporting and to fasten the recording of financial transactions.

We recommended and agreed that Management instructs the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal voucher to recognize assets and property, plant, and equipment, and a credit to Government Equity account as follows:
Other PPE						80,725.00
Government Equity					80,725.00
Additionally, instruct the Municipal Accountant to observe the provisions of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs for fair presentation of the financial statements.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to forward the monthly report of unpaid water bills to the Accounting Office to recognize income and receivable accounts; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to follow the accrual basis of recording revenues, and to prepare journal voucher for the understatement of Receivables and Government Equity accounts to present fairly its financial statements at year-end.

We recommended and agreed that Management religiously comply with all the reportorial requirements for the utilization of public funds, such as BGCM, to attain a higher degree of transparency of information and adhere to the oversight committees' full disclosure policy.

We recommended that Management strictly observed the rules and regulations for the utilization of the BGCM. Additionally, instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit additional documents pertaining to the payment of vehicle rentals and reimbursement of incidental expenses to determine its propriety and regularity. Further, submit the liquidation documents for payment of incidental expenses.

We recommended and agreed that the Local Chief Executive instructs the Municipal Treasurer to issue Inventory Custodian Slip and Property Acknowledgement Receipt for inventory and PPE items issued to prevent losses of government properties.

We recommended and agreed that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the JMC for the payment of benefits to its PHWs, particularly the computation for the entitlement of Laundry and Subsistence Allowance.

We recommended and agreed that the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant the submission of daily time records to those personnel who did not submit the same and the alternative work arrangements during the period of ECQ. Additionally, strictly observed the provisions of DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-01 for the computation and payment of CoVID 19 Hazard Pay.
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the necessary details of the Loan Payable.

We reiterated our recommendation from the previous year audit that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the accounting unit with the certified list of taxpayers with the stated amount due and collectible for the year as basis in setting-up the RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of each year.

We recommended that Management implement the development projects programmed under the 20% development fund at the earliest possible time to ensure the early completion of the project and to strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DBM and DILG and ensure its optimal utilization.
We recommended the management to stop the practice of granting cash advance to elected officials other than for travelling expenses according to COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct Municipal Accountant to follow strictly the guidelines on the documentary requirements for government transactions, particularly on the granting cash advances for payroll.

We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014, in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO's organizational structure and manpower complement aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that claims are duly supported with required documents before processing payments. Further, direct the submission of the required documents for further verification.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher for each transaction recorded in the General Journals and submit it to the Office of the Auditor together with the proper/necessary supporting documents.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to focus on ways of improving the collection efficiency of the Municipality in order to generate enough funds to augment its limited resources.
We recommended to Management to revisit the Municipal Market Code of 2016 and strictly observe the implementation of the Ordinance.

We recommended the Management to require the Accountant to strictly comply with the provision of RA 9679 in observing the maximum limit of PAG-IBIG contribution government share at ₱100 per month per employee.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer furnish the accounting unit with the certified list of taxpayers with the stated amount due and collectible for the year to enable to set-up the correct RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of each year and make necessary adjusting entries.

observations.recommendation: 1.5	We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary reconciliation on the reciprocal accounts and take up necessary adjusting entries to correct the reported balances.

2.4	We recommend that Management instruct the inventory committee to prepare the Report on Physical Count of PPE and submit to the Office of the Team Leader. Likewise, conduct the reconciliation of physical inventory against the property and accounting records.
3.7      We recommended that the Management communicate with the Supplier and demand payment for the liquidated damages.

4.7	We recommend strict adherence to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

5.3	We recommended that Management direct the accounting office to maintain complete subsidiary ledgers and prepare the corresponding financial statements to each special accounts to form part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance to serve as basis of the management to evaluate and recommend corrective measures for the improvement of the economic enterprises.

6.5	We recommended strict adherence to the provision of Section 37 of Republic Act No. 9003 on the use of Sanitary Landfill for the people of the Municipality to enjoy the benefits for which this Act was created.

7.7	We recommended that the Management enhance the monitoring of timely implementation of the projects, proper planning and coordination with the concerned officials for an efficient and economical utilization of government funds and to attain the desired objective of the projects.

We recommended that management stop the granting of honoraria to the members of the Liga ng mga Barangay and strictly follow the guidelines on the granting of honoraria to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We recommend that management re-visit the guidelines set forth under COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 particularly on the identification of programs/projects chargeable to Peace and Order Program, since this is the basis in the computation of the 30% allocation for confidential fund of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management: 

Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) as one of the basic document supporting fund transfers pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2012-001. 

Record all fund transfer as Receivables and require the Leagues to submit liquidation/utilization report to monitor the utilization of the fund.

We recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the proper utilization of LDRRMF with reference to Item 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 for the projects and activities chargeable against the LDRRMF.

We recommended that management submit explanation/justification on our observation for further verification and for final audit disposition. Refrain from entering similar kind of transaction in the future.

We recommended that management direct the Inventory committee together with the property and accounting units to prepare the reconciliation of the result of the inventory count with the Property and Accounting records.
observations.managementAction: Some records are difficult to retrieve and/or no longer obtainable.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to direct the inventory committee and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancy between the accounting records and the Report on the Physical Count of PPE.
observations.reason:
Not implemented.
Reiterated in current AAR.
Management failed to prepare the reconciliation on the result of inventory count against the property and accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to direct the BAC Secretariat, thru the BAC Chairperson to prepare the procurement monitoring report of the Municipality and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance to Section 12.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend management to direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund and to prepare separate financial reports on the operation of the municipal water system pursuant to Section 313 of RA 7160. We also recommend management to pass and adopt a resolution authorizing the opening of a separate bank account for the municipal water system.

1.6	We recommended that management stop the granting of honoraria to the members of the Liga ng mga Barangay and strictly follow the guidelines on the granting of honoraria to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

2.4	We recommend that management re-visit the guidelines set forth under COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 particularly on the identification of programs/projects chargeable to Peace and Order Program, since this is the basis in the computation of the 30% allocation for confidential fund of the LGU.

3.3	We recommended that management strictly adhere to the guidelines on the identification of eligible projects for funding under the confidential fund to avoid the granting of cash advances in excess of the authorized amount.

4.6	We recommend that the Local School Board be guided by the provisions of Sec. 100 (c) of RA 7160 in the identification of priority expenditures chargeable to SEF and in the preparation of its annual budget.

5.3	We recommend that the Municipal Accountant follow strictly the rules and regulations on the grant of compensation-related Magna Carta benefits to Public Health Workers based on their daily time records.

6.5	We recommended that the Municipal Development Council formulate and prepare the Annual Investment Plan in consonance with the development goals of the LGU geared towards the implementation of socio-development programs that will ultimately uplift the living condition of its people.

7.4	We recommend that the management direct the Municipal Development Council to specify the programs, projects and activities funded by the 20% Development Fund to ensure that the projects are implemented within the scheduled time frame.

8.4	We recommend strict adherence to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

9.4	We recommend that management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) as one of the basic document supporting fund transfers pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2012-001.
Record all fund transfer as Receivables and require the Leagues to submit liquidation/utilization report to monitor the utilization of the fund.

10.6	We recommended strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the proper utilization of LDRRMF with reference to Item 5 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 for the projects and activities chargeable against the LDRRMF.

11.5	We recommended that management submit explanation/justification on our observation for further verification and for final audit disposition. Refrain from entering similar kind of transaction in the future.

12.5	We recommended that management direct the Inventory committee together with the property and accounting units to prepare the reconciliation of the result of the inventory count with the Property and Accounting records.
We recommend to direct the inventory committee and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the discrepancy between the accounting records and the Report on the Physical Count of PPE.
observations.reason:
Not implemented.
Reiterated in current AAR.
Management failed to prepare the reconciliation on the result of inventory count against the property and accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the provisions of Item 3.2 of COA Circular No. 87-278 dated November 12, 1987 on the submission of perfected contracts for infrastructure projects to the Office of the Auditor within the specified period.
observations.managementAction: Comply with  the specified period for submission of perfected contracts

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 before a recommendation for the award of contract to the Head of the Procuring Entity. Otherwise, the lowest calculated bid will be disqualified for the award.

We recommend the management to direct the BAC Secretariat, thru the BAC Chairperson to prepare the procurement monitoring report of the Municipality and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance to Section 12.2 of Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend strict compliance with the provisions of the COA, DBM, DILG, GCG and DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015 which provided the guidelines in the entitlement, release, use, reporting and audit of confidential and/or intelligence funds.

We recommend management to direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund and to prepare separate financial reports on the operation of the municipal water system pursuant to Section 313 of RA 7160. We also recommend management to pass and adopt a resolution authorizing the opening of a separate bank account for the municipal water system.

We recommend that the LGU strictly comply with the provisions of item3.1 of DBM Budget Circular (BC) No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007 on the grant of honoraria to government personnel involved in government procurement and/or request an authority from DOH-Regional Office No. XIII to use the amount allocated for engineering supervision, contingency and miscellaneous items in the POW for the payment of honorarium and overtime fee of the LGU personnel involved in the procurement activities.
We recommend that the LGU strictly comply with the provisions of Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) 9184.
